title: crepis taraxacifolia subsp. haenseleri
intro: Crepis taraxacifolia subsp. haenseleri, also known as Haensel's Hawksbeard, is a plant species in the dandelion-like genus Crepis. This plant is believed to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe wounds and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Crepis taraxacifolia subsp. haenseleri include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
